extra.reasoning: The property forbids (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) both true. rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), which requires ALL six of heart_failure, anticoagulated, seizure_history, immunosuppressed, polypharmacy, diabetes to be true; otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. Therefore rec_drug_a = true implies exactly that conjunction holds. In that same state, rec_drug_b is governed by two matching rules: r1 (priority 90) sets rec_drug_b := false under the identical six-condition conjunction, and r4 (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true under heart_failure. Since conflict resolution picks the highest-priority matching rule per output, r1 (priority 90) beats r4 (priority 50), forcing rec_drug_b := false. Thus in every state where rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b is necessarily false. There is no reachable input (prior_gi_bleed is irrelevant to a/b) making both true. The property holds over the entire input space.
